content.content:
# Concrete Claims from "Designing Bento Grids That Actually Work"

**Publication Date:** 2026 (article references current year)

## Falsifiable Claims

**Market Adoption:**
- "67% of the top 100 SaaS products on ProductHunt" now use bento grids

**User Performance:**
- "Tasks are completed 23% faster with modular layouts compared to traditional linear designs"
- Eye tracking studies show users "fixate 2.6× longer on larger grid items, regardless of position"

## Design Specifications

**Card Size Hierarchy:**
- 1×1 box (100px × 100px): Supporting features, secondary actions
- 2×1 box (216px × 100px): Mid-tier features with horizontal emphasis
- 2×2 box (216px × 216px): Hero feature, primary value proposition
- 3×2 box (332px × 216px): Detailed feature demonstrations

**Standard Gutter Sizes:**
- Desktop: 16-24px
- Tablet: 12-16px
- Mobile: 12px

**Card Internal Structure:**
- Visual element: 60-70% of card height
- Headline: 20-30% of card height (1-6 words maximum)
- Supporting text: 10-20% (1-2 lines maximum)

**Responsive Breakpoints:**
- Desktop (1200px+): 3-4 columns
- Tablet (768-1199px): 2 columns
- Mobile (<768px): 1 column

**Interaction Timing:**
- Hover transitions: 200-300ms ease-out
- Active state scale: 0.98× for 100ms duration
